title: Encounter Tactics
description: How the enemy fights — original table authored for the encounter builder.
entries: They open from ambush, striking the {pick:rearmost|most heavily armored|least armored|most obviously magical} target first.
They fight in relays — half attack while half fall back to recover, trading places every few moments.
They focus everything on one target at a time and ignore all others until it drops.
They try to split the party, luring {pick:the fastest|the bravest|the most curious} member away before the real attack.
They target mounts, torches, and packs before people — chaos first, blood second.
They use the terrain: attacking from {pick:high ground|dense cover|shallow water|narrow choke points}, retreating through it when pressed.
They are defending something {pick:behind them|beneath the ground here|hidden nearby} and will not pursue past it.
They begin at range and melt away rather than accept melee, resetting the skirmish again and again.
One of them is clearly in charge; the rest break and scatter {pick:the moment it falls|if it flees|if it is grappled or silenced}.
They feign a rout after the first exchange, drawing pursuers into {pick:a second group|a trap|worse ground}.
They fight to wound, not to kill — they want {pick:prisoners|food they can drag away|trophies|slaves}.
They coordinate with signals: {pick:whistles|drumbeats|hand signs|colored smoke}. Disrupting the signaler disrupts everything.
They swarm the spellcaster the instant the first spell goes off.
They attack in waves — a third now, a third when battle is joined, the rest only if winning.
They shove, grapple, and drag rather than strike, trying to pull someone {pick:off a ledge|into the water|into the dark|away from the light}.
They are terrified and cornered; they fight desperately but take any honest opening to flee.
They test the party first — one probing attack, then withdrawal to watch how the group responds.
They protect their weakest member ferociously; threatening it enrages them and breaks their discipline.
They fight dirty: {pick:sand in the eyes|nets and hooks|caltrops behind them|feigned surrender}.
They wait for the party to engage something else first, then hit the flank.
They herd rather than fight, pushing the party toward {pick:a cliff|a lair|a snare line|another monster's territory}.
They will not cross {pick:running water|a line of old standing stones|firelight|consecrated ground}, and they know the party doesn't know that.
They stalk at the edge of vision for a long while first; the attack comes only when someone straggles.
They simply charge, immediately, all at once, screaming.
